perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) Live-action video. A young woman wearing a dark beret and a blue off-the-shoulder top stands in a Costco, holding a large container of Garlic Seafood Spaghetti. (2) Close-ups show the deli price tag of 455 TWD/kg and the product label. (3) In a kitchen, the pasta is removed from the kit and weighed on a digital scale, displaying 408g. (4) Hands use tongs to stir-fry garlic slices, chili, and seafood (squid, shrimp, clams) in a stainless steel pan. (5) The noodles are added to the pan and seasoned with an electric pepper mill and salt. (6) A woman in a purple top tosses the pasta. (7) The finished dish is plated. (8) Two women sit at a table in a home setting with plush toys behind them, tasting the food and giving a negative review using hand gestures and facial expressions of disappointment.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, mid-tempo electronic background music plays throughout. A female voice speaks with high energy about finding the pasta at Costco. (2) Sizzling sounds of stir-frying and the mechanical whirring of an electric pepper mill are heard. A male voice (off-screen) provides encouraging commentary like "Smells so good" and "It's served!". (3) The audio shifts to two female voices at a dining table, their tone becoming more analytical and critical as they describe the texture and lack of flavor. (4) Ambient kitchen and dining room noises are present throughout.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: Subtitles translate the spoken Mandarin dialogue. (2) Decorative/Hook Text: "455元值得?" (Is 455 yuan worth it?) and "香蒜海鮮義大利麵" (Garlic Seafood Spaghetti) serve as hooks. (3) Emotional/Variety Text: "哇給超多料" (Wow, so many ingredients), "有妹妹真好!" (Good to have a sister!), and "可以路過" (You can pass/skip this) which conveys their final verdict.

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Shopping discovery -> Home cooking process -> Final tasting and critical review

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 female creators visible; 1 off-camera male voice heard but not seen.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: One woman has a dark Kangol beret and a blue top; the second woman wears a purple top and later appears with the first woman during the review.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: Costco deli section with refrigerated shelves; a modern kitchen with an induction cooktop and stainless steel cookware; a home dining area with various plush toys and a 'BT21' pillow.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Bright indoor lighting, likely natural mixed with home/store overhead lights.

vocal_qualities.tone: Energetic and curious initially, shifting to critical and unimpressed
vocal_qualities.pitch: Mixed (High for females, Deep for male)

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a consumer-focused food review intended to evaluate the value-for-money of a Costco prepared meal kit. By showing the actual weight, the preparation steps, and the final taste results, the creators provide a 'buyer beware' message, ultimately advising viewers that the product is mediocre and not worth the high price.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The review is overwhelmingly negative, recommending that viewers 'pass' on the product.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Use of variety-show style dynamic captions and a multi-speaker narrative (onscreen sisters + offscreen male voice).
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video follows the 'expectation vs. reality' trope common in influencer food reviews, using empirical evidence (weighing the food) to support their critique.
